sanskrit: मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्ितयोगेन सेवते । स गुणान्समतीत्यैतान् ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥
hindiTranslation: जो मनुष्य अव्यभिचारी भक्तियोगके द्वारा मेरा सेवन करता है, वह इन गुणोंका अतिक्रमण करके ब्रह्मप्राप्तिका पात्र हो जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: Whoever serves Me with unwavering devotion transcends these qualities and becomes fit for union with the supreme reality.
essence: Steady devotion can carry you beyond the forces that shape ordinary life.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Steady devotion to Krishna lifts a person beyond the three qualities of nature and makes that person fit for union with the supreme reality.
simpleInsight: Love that does not waver can take you past the limits of your ordinary nature.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is still trapped in doubt while Krishna explains how to rise above the forces that bind human behavior. This verse gives the turning point: unwavering devotion to Krishna is the way beyond the three qualities and into the highest reality.
modernRelevance: You keep trying to fix yourself through sheer will, but the deeper shift comes when your whole heart stays aligned. That steady devotion changes what you can become.
translationLiteral: मां (Me) / च (and) / यः (who) / अव्यभिचारेण (with unwavering) / भक्ति-योगेन (through devotion-yoga) / सेवते (serves) / सः (that one) / गुणान् (the qualities) / समतीत्य (having fully transcended) / एतान् (these) / ब्रह्म-भूयाय (for becoming Brahman) / कल्पते (is fit)
insight: It shifts the goal from self-improvement alone to sustained devotion as the force that transforms nature.

quoteVariants.text: Serve Me with unwavering love. You rise beyond the three qualities.
Love fully. Rise beyond the qualities.
Devotion carries you where effort alone cannot.
Unwavering devotion lifts you beyond nature.
